title: Rotavirus deaths prevented by vaccination
subtitle: The number of [rotavirus](#dod:rotavirus) deaths of children under five years old that were prevented in 2016 due to the rotavirus vaccine.
caption:
Rotavirus deaths prevented by vaccination, 2016

The number of rotavirus deaths of children under five years old that were prevented in 2016 due to the rotavirus vaccine.

This choropleth map displays the estimated number of rotavirus deaths prevented among children under five years old in 2016, using a color scale ranging from pale yellow for zero prevented deaths to dark blue for values exceeding 3,000. The highest concentrations of prevented deaths appear across sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia, where the darkest blue shading indicates figures above 3,000 in nations such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, and India. China also falls within this highest band, showing a substantial number of prevented fatalities. Moving to the middle of the scale, countries in the 300 to 1,000 range include Brazil, Mexico, Pakistan, and Indonesia, represented by medium teal tones. Lighter shades of green and yellow-green cover nations like the United States, Russia, and Australia, placing them in the lower bands between 0 and 30 prevented deaths. Large portions of the map, particularly in Greenland, central Asia, and scattered island nations, are marked with hatching to denote that no data is available for those regions. The visual distribution highlights a clear geographic divide, with the greatest absolute numbers of lives saved concentrated in populous developing regions, while wealthier nations with lower baseline mortality or different disease burdens show significantly lower counts on this specific metric. The data shows that the burden of rotavirus mortality averted by vaccination in 2016 was heavily skewed toward high-population countries in Africa and Asia.
